title: Level 171
wikitext:
{{Notice|body=This page is highly recommended to be viewed in dark mode!}}
{{Foreground|File=Static5i.gif|Opacity=0.1}}{{Headerbox|header=''The wind hums a song that you almost remember.''|file=Nightnungs.png|speed=5}} {{Class Custom Image||bg-image=Backgroundpage.png|class=Ψ|hexagon=Ψ|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Psychological Hazards}} __NOTOC__ == Description == {{Blurimage|caption=An image of Level 171.|align=right|file=57.png.png}}'''Level 171''', aptly named "'''Barren Baseplate,'''" is a large concrete rooftop situated within a vast colorless cityscape. The rooftop is barren and lifeless, with no notable aspects on the rooftop, spanning 5km wide and 5km long of nothing but concrete floors, making it one of the only levels to have a limit, due to the cityscape being impossible to access. The level is quite colorless compared to other levels, though brighter colors can still be noticed in the sky or items. In the distance, the cityscape is made up of varying sizes of skyscrapers most of them shorter than the rooftop, with taller skyscrapers still noticeable. The buildings show signs of life in them, light can be observed through the windows, cycling through off and on as if someone is living in them, no living entities or wanderers have ever been seen through the windows. The rooftop's floor is made up of smooth concrete tiles making up it's surface; arranged in a grid, with some tiles being darker than others. Rarely, an entrance to a long '''stairwell''' can be found among the emptiness, extending far deeper than the actual length of the building, which can be observed when reaching the edge of the rooftop. Additionally, the '''stairwell''' houses the only known light sources other than items in the entire level, having lights at the end of every flight of stairs. The level is lit up by it's own sun, similar to the sun observed in the '''[[The Frontrooms]]'''; however, it is drastically colder to prevent the level from scorching itself due to the absence of a day and night cycle, this also results in a cold wind that is constantly blowing throughout the level, reminiscent of a fan on its lowest level, due to this it is recommended to bring minor warm clothing to the level due to wind and the sun providing no warmth. The level is perpetuated by a faint static obscuring the vision of wanderers, with complete silence around the rooftop, only broken by the occasional sound of civilization from the city or the sound of an airliner flying overhead, Looking at the source of any of these sounds reveals no sign of what could've made them. Wanderers who enter the level report the humid scent of warm asphalt after rain, with a hint of sweetness; this smell remains constant throughout the level, unaffected by the wind or outside sources. When exploring this level, wanderers report frequent sight distortion, the most noticeable and constant example being an inability to focus on anything, as if the level is always in the background. Other examples include fast movement like fast moving objects leaving lasting afterimages, shadows moving faster or slower, and aspects of the level shifting if they are not watched. No other wanderers can be found in this level, completely separating the wanderer in it.{{Blurimage|file=Miller57.png|caption=A signed photo of '''Level 171''', the signature reads, "Miller."}}Strangely, the level is constantly removing memories from the wanderer's mind as they explore it, this can be explained via the properties of many different chemicals within the air of this level, though other effects seem to be anomalous in nature. The constant memory loss complements the shifting environment, as wanderers are not able to remember if anything within the level changed or if it was always there. Due to these properties, multiple effects can be observed, these effects include wanderers stopping momentarily, overcome by momentary confusion, forgetting entirely what they were in the level for, or what their goal was, similar to brain fog, a wanderer suddenly forgetting their name while writing reminders, a sense of déjà vu once in the level, and variable distortions in time; a minute could feel like an hour, or the complete opposite. Additionally, other than these effects, noises made within the level are delayed; this effect occasionally slowed down the speed of sound itself, this was observed using thrown objects from a wanderer. Additionally, wanderers reported forgetting all experiences of the level shortly after leaving; this made documentation of the level when first discovered exceedingly difficult, as it could only be documented by experienced wanderers, due to the only way to get any information back being to write reminders of the level's aspects while in it, or quickly write down the level's information when exiting it. == Chemical Composition == A plethora of chemical compounds and elements can be found within the level's air, albeit at a low concentration which contribute to the levels anomalous properties as said before, with one contributing the most, and having the highest presence within the level, that being '''nitrous oxide''' gas. No source as to where this gas originates from has been found within the level at this time. '''Nitrous oxide''' (N₂O) is a colorless gas at room temperature, with a smell that has a faint odor of sweetness, and fresh hay or mild chemicals, this gas is heavier than air which results in low amounts being found on the rooftop, though still enough to induce its effects on wanderers over a long period of time, these effects explain a multitude of the level's properties. The effects of '''Nitrous oxide''' compose of a similar feeling to the effects of anesthetic on a human, disassociation, euphoria, an altered perception of time and space, out of body sensations; akin to floating or being in a dream, Brief confusion, and short term memory loss. Below is a more specific depiction of its effects, as well as its effects within the rarely occurring '''stairwell'''. Info collected by '''[[M.E.G.]]''' agents. {| class="article-table" ! style="background-color: #36454F" |Conditions
Conditions
! style="background-color: #36454F" |Oxygen to '''Nitrous Oxide''' level
Oxygen to '''Nitrous Oxide''' level
! style="background-color: #36454F" |Effect on the body
Effect on the body
|- | style="background-color: #0" |Mild oxygen displacement, can be found on the rooftop. | style="background-color: #0" |Oxygen levels drop below 40% | style="background-color: #0" |Dizziness, trouble focusing, and short term memory loss. |- | style="background-color: #0" |Oxygen deficient environment, can be found deep into the stairwell. | style="background-color: #0" |Oxygen level drops below 19.5% | style="background-color: #0" |Possible risk of unconsciousness, body sends no warning signs to the brain, aswell as feelings of safety overcoming the wanderer. |- | style="background-color: #0" |High concentration of '''nitrous oxide''', can be found at the bottom of the '''stairwell'''. | style="background-color: #0" |Nitrous levels exceed 50%. | style="background-color: #0" |Unconsciousness sets in about a minute, feelings of euphoria overcome the wanderer, unable to recognize their slowly dying body. |- | style="background-color: #0" |1-3 months on the rooftop of the level. | style="background-color: #0" |'''N2O''' > 100ppm. (Long term exposure.) | style="background-color: #0" |Long term memory loss, Numbness, Disassociation, and Chronic Neurological damage. |- |} == The Stairwell == {{Blurimage|file=Stair57.png|caption=A photo found deep into the stairwell, photographer presumed dead.}}A critical amount of '''nitrous oxide''' can be found in the '''stairwell''', which itself can be entered by finding a rare door far out from the center of the rooftop, the door of the '''stairwell''' has a window which can be observed to show condensation on the other side of it, indicating a critical amount of '''nitrous oxide''' gas on the other side. The danger of the gas is due to the enclosed space within the '''stairwell''' which can induce unconsciousness, hallucinations, and rarely asphyxiation, as the '''nitrous oxide''' will slowly replace the oxygen within the '''stairwell''', however, the wanderer will not feel the sensation instantly due to the properties of nitrous. These effects only persist deeper into the '''stairwell''' as nitrous tends to pool at the bottom due to it having denser-than-air properties, specifically being 1.375 times denser than oxygen in molecular weight. Effects closer to the beginning of the '''stairwell''' include false comfort and mental deterioration; evolving into feelings of safety, calm, and dreariness while the brains neural receptors numb from exposure to nitrous oxide as the body descends deeper. At the bottom of the stairwell is a breaker box that controls the level's power and can be switched off to cut the lights across the whole level. As an effect of low oxygen content in the blood (Hypoxemia),the wanderer's skin will begin to turn blue, feeling of disorientation and confusion will ensue, they will hyperventilate and their heart will begin to palpitate, leading to organ failure or comatose, shortly followed by death as in this state the wanderer is unable to utilize basic motor functions. Staying in the level for around 1-3 months, or as little as 10-30 minutes depending on your depth in the level, is enough to be considered chronic exposure to the gas. The full list of effects of chronic exposure to '''nitrous oxide''' and hypoxemia are as follows: COPD (Chronic Hypoxemia), lasting memory damage, cognitive impairment, headaches, confusion, coma, seizures, and hallucinations. If exposure is left untreated it can lead to paralysis of the body as well. Due to this, it is highly recommended to not use this level as a place to settle due to the long lasting effects that result due to prolonged time in the level. The '''stairwell''' is the only part of the level allowing items such as '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''' to spawn, thus, the level can be used to restock on supplies via the '''stairwell''', though it is not recommended to stay in the level for any more than an hour without proper protection like a gas mask to suffer minimal damage to cognitive functions. == Additional Information == === Equipment === Before entering this level, there are a few things to note. Bringing a gas mask to expeditions into the '''stairwell''' is recommended, however, remember that the gas permeating the level is not the only mental hazard present within it, so limiting time in the '''stairwell''' to around an hour is a good idea. Bringing an inhaler too is a fool proof method to prevent respiratory damage when used after leaving the '''stairwell''' or leaving the level entirely. == Weather == Every 4 hours, the level will cycle between clear skies or differing weather events. |-| Storm= The clouds above will darken and a shower of rain will begin falling onto the level , causing a smell of wet asphalt to rise from the roads beneath the rooftop and invoking a strange sense of nostalgia within the wanderer. An increased rate of memory loss is present during this. Additionally, this event is able to transform into a thunderstorm without indication. |-|Thunderstorm= This event has no initial indication to signal its arrival, and instead may start during a storm. Thunder bolts will shoot down from the darkened clouds and cut power to the level given enough time to strike the correct area, shutting off all lights in the level and leaving it pitch black, aside from the occasional flash of light from the thunder. This effect can be reversed using a breaker box at the bottom of the stairwell, although this method provides little reward for such a high risk factor. Waiting out the event will return light to the level via its sun, although power will remain cut until the breaker is flipped, or until 3-7 hours pass. If the darkness is waited out, the level will have restructured itself by the time light returns, the rooftop may be slighter longer, or shorter, and the buildings around it will have different lights turned on, or be replaced by different buildings. All memories of the level before the blackout will change themselves to show the "restructured" level instead of the correct version. |-| Snaw= Although rare, thick chunks of chewy snow will gently fall onto the rooftop and the city beyond it from a veil of white clouds, covering the rooftop relatively quick compared to snowfall in the '''The Frontrooms''', rising up to about bit below knee level which can make traversing the level extremely difficult due to the texture of the snow, although this does have some benefits as the snow will smother the layer of nitrous oxide pooling on the floor of the rooftop, and build up on the entrance of the stairwell, making long term exploration of the level quite manageable. It is possible to be trapped within the stairwell by the buildup of snow, which can be life-threatening. If a chunk of snow is caught before it hits the ground, it is edible and has a texture comparable to badly made icecream, while the taste more closely resembles plain shaved ice. This delicacy was lazily dubbed '''Snaw''' by the first few wanderers to discover the level, and can be taken out of the level if left in a cooler. |-| Fog= Commonly, a dense fog will descend onto the rooftop, enough to block a wanderers view of the surrounding city and obscure their path through the rooftop. It is advised to not take rests during the fog or change directions in any way, as the fog will cause a "restructuring" of parts of the level when said parts are unobserved, ignoring the visual impairment imposed by the fog, in other words, when the fog lifts the rooftop may be smaller or longer, while the surrounding city will include new buildings and the lack of old ones, although the observed path will appear unchanged when the fog lifts, to the point of seeming out of place to the rest of the rooftop. The line between the observed and unobserved path will blur together, as if it were a memory intruding in a mind it wasn't meant for. Additionally memories of the old unobserved path will be damaged, and eventually removed by the levels anomalous properties given enough time. == Entities == This level is devoid of entities. == Colonies and Outposts == No colonies or outposts are present on this level due to the mental hazards and nitrous oxide gas permeating the level, as well as the lack of resources to build an outpost. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === When sleeping on the rooftop of a tower in '''[[Level 510]]''' the wanderer will be transported to this level. Finding a stairwell entrance on a roof in '''[[Level 11]]''' will lead the wanderer here. === Exits === • When reaching the edge of the rooftop and jumping off, clouds will form above the streets below, which when entered will transport the wanderer to '''[[Level 97]]'''. Opening a door at the bottom of the '''stairwell''' will lead to '''[[The Void]]'''.
'''Author''':'''[[User:Thallseye|Thallseye]]'''
